# Copilot instructions for undercutspiky.github.io

## Repository overview

This is a **Jekyll** static site deployed on **GitHub Pages**.
It is a personal deep-learning research notebook styled with **Tailwind CSS v3** and
hand-written CSS custom properties (CSS variables).

## Non-negotiable constraints

- **Do not replace Jekyll** with React, Next.js, Astro, Gatsby, or any JavaScript framework.
- **Preserve all existing URLs.** Blog post URLs follow `/YYYY/MM/DD/slug.html`. Never
  change a `permalink`, file name, or date prefix of a published post.
- **Never fabricate scientific information.** Do not invent author names, publication
  details, experimental results, metrics, or DOIs. Use `TODO` markers for unknown values.
- **Maintain visual consistency** with the existing djay.lab dark-navy design.
  Colours, typography, navigation styling, and card styling must not change without
  explicit instruction.

## Build commands

Always verify these pass before completing any task:

```bash
# Install Node dependencies (Tailwind CSS CLI)
npm ci

# Build minified CSS  ← must produce assets/css/site.css
npm run build

# Build Jekyll site (fails on Liquid errors)
bundle exec jekyll build --strict_front_matter
```

## CSS architecture

- **Source**: `assets/index.css` — edit this file.
- **Output**: `assets/css/site.css` — generated by `npm run build`; **do not edit manually**.
- Styles are plain CSS classes (`.hero`, `.nav-link`, `.post-card`, etc.).
- Design tokens are CSS custom properties defined in `:root` (e.g. `var(--teal)`, `var(--navy-900)`).
- Tailwind utilities are only lightly used; do not blindly add Tailwind utility classes
  to templates — use the existing CSS class system instead.
- After editing `assets/index.css`, always run `npm run build` and commit the updated
  `assets/css/site.css`.

## Content types

| Location | Purpose |
|----------|---------|
| `_posts/` | Blog articles, commentary, paper summaries, informal writing |
| `_research/` | Own publications, research projects, methods, datasets |

These content types must remain separate. Blog posts may reference research entries via
`research: [slug]` front matter. They must never be merged or cross-contaminated.

## Jekyll collection — research

Defined in `_config.yml`. Items are published at `/research/<slug>/`.

Front matter schema: see `_research/shape-based-feature-learning.md` for a full example.
All fields are optional except `layout`, `title`, `slug`, and `date`.
Templates must not render empty buttons, labels, or sections for missing fields.

## Layouts and includes

| File | Used for |
|------|----------|
| `_layouts/default.html` | Every page wrapper (nav, footer) |
| `_layouts/blog_post.html` | Blog post articles |
| `_layouts/research_item.html` | Individual research item pages |
| `_includes/research_card.html` | Research card used on listing and homepage |

## Navigation order

Home → Research → Blog → About  
(defined in `_layouts/default.html`)

## GitHub Actions CI

`.github/workflows/ci.yml` runs on every push and PR:
1. `npm ci`
2. `npm run build` + CSS size check
3. `bundle install` + `jekyll build --strict_front_matter`
4. Verify output pages exist

Pull-request builds do **not** deploy to GitHub Pages.

## Deployment

GitHub Pages deploys directly from the `master` branch.
`assets/css/site.css` is committed to the repository (required for Pages to serve it).

## Analytics

Google Analytics tag ID `G-CCNJ72T8D0` is embedded in `_layouts/default.html`.
**Do not commit new secrets, tokens, or credentials.**
